sources.issuing_organisation: International Society for Holter and Noninvasive Electrocardiology and Heart Rhythm Society
sources.exact_document_title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
sources.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
sources.clinical_setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting symptoms, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, monitor type and duration context, recording quality, and clinician review of Holter or ambulatory ECG data. It is old and does not generate a rhythm diagnosis, monitor order, treatment, or referral.

exact_sections.heading: Section 3.1.6 - selection of appropriate ambulatory ECG technology
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 4.1-4.2 - syncope and palpitations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations table - selection of ambulatory ECG
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting monitor type and duration in relation to symptom frequency and recording window; treatment and diagnosis are outside scope.

archive.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
archive.publisher: International Society for Holter and Noninvasive Electrocardiology and Heart Rhythm Society
archive.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
archive.setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Section 3.1.6 - selection of appropriate ambulatory ECG technology
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Sections 4.1-4.2 - syncope and palpitations
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1-1.4.3 - stroke and bleeding risk, cardiac function, and personalised care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led review of stroke and bleeding risk, comorbidities, echocardiography context, symptoms, patient preferences, information, adherence, and a documented care package.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1-1.6.17 and 1.11.1-1.11.2 - anticoagulation discussion and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-performed benefit-risk discussion, adherence and control review, renal and bleeding context, annual reassessment, and documented decisions without generating a medicine choice or dose.
